category_title: Verbal pupils who ignore or protest adult-led interactions
minimal_context: The student and adult are sitting together for a 1:1 session using a bottle of bubbles to encourage language use.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Here are the bubbles for our lesson. What is inside this bottle?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Yes. Now, tell me what I should do with the wand.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You need to use a full sentence first. Say 'blow the bubbles'.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Turns away from the adult and ignores the instruction) No.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Here are the bubbles for our lesson. What is inside this bottle?

STUDENT: Bubbles.

TUTOR: Yes. Now, tell me what I should do with the wand.

STUDENT: (Reaches for the bottle) Want.

TUTOR: You need to use a full sentence first. Say 'blow the bubbles'.

STUDENT: (Turns away from the adult and ignores the instruction) No.

task.content: Participating in a 'bubble' lesson during a 1:1 interaction session
task.learning_goal: To get so much more language from him

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the adult was rushing him, forcing him, or using directive communication
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Ignoring or protesting at what the adults did or said, and working away on his own agenda
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Did not like demands and expectations placed on them
related_quotes: I was forcing him to, yeah, I know I can see it now . . . So, I have to wait, but he can talk I think because he can talk I can see now I am rushing him.

behavior_patterns.condition: When adults rely mainly on 'test questions', 'verbal prompts' and 'yes/no' type questions
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Heard in one-word responses mainly; shows no interest in interacting with adults
